title: Use seccomp_profile as default profile if defined in containers.conf
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

I bumped into the issue that pointing `seccomp_profile` in containers.conf to a user-defined profile completely ignored my settings. Instead, podman kept using the profile in `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`. This PR fixes that issue, by considering the runtime configuration of higher priority than the default settings.

comments.body: Checks are failing due to the lack of tests. Should I actually add tests for this small fix? I guess it could be nice to add one test to avoid future regressions, but not sure where exactly to do so.

I would prefer this patch, and then add a test to make sure info shows the correct path.
[seccomp.patch](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/6387320/seccomp.patch.txt)

Thank you for your fast reply! That would make sense. I also took more time than necessary to figure out that podman was using the wrong path, which this should fix. I'll apply it and figure out the tests for it.
@rhatdan tests are fixed now. I skipped container and remote tests, as I could not see they would add any value.

How about this for the test to test/e2e/containers_conf_test.go
```
	It("podman info seccomp profile path", func() {
		SkipIfInContainer("Storage needs to be configured")
		configPath := filepath.Join(podmanTest.TempDir, "containers.conf")
		os.Setenv("CONTAINERS_CONF", configPath)

		profile := filepath.Join(podmanTest.TempDir, "seccomp.json")
		containersConf := []byte(fmt.Sprintf("[containers]\nseccomp_profile=\"%s\"", profile))
		err = ioutil.WriteFile(configPath, containersConf, os.ModePerm)
		Expect(err).To(BeNil())

		if IsRemote() {
			podmanTest.RestartRemoteService()
		}

		session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"info", "--format", "{{.Host.Security.SECCOMPProfilePath}}"})
		session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(session.ExitCode()).To(Equal(0))
		Expect(session.OutputToString()).To(Equal(profile))
	})
```

/approve
LGTM, once the test is replaced.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *pablofsf*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan, it is added now, although I have removed the `SkipIfInContainer`, as I understand that using `podmanTest.Podman` then that is unnecessary.

Also, it has my `Signed-off by:`, but feels like it shouldn't, as you actually implemented all the changes :)

Don't worry, I am fine with you getting all the credit.  Thanks for bringing this to our attention.
LGTM
